rationale: This reaction exemplifies the Schlosser modification of the Wittig reaction to achieve E-selectivity with a non-stabilized ylide. Propanal reacts with the ylide derived from ethyltrimethylphosphonium. The key steps involve deprotonation of the initial betaine intermediate with a second equivalent of organolithium (methyllithium) to form a lithiobetaine, which equilibrates to the more stable trans-lithiobetaine. Subsequent protonation (e.g., with t-butanol) and elimination yield the (E)-hex-3-ene product with high stereoselectivity.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses simple, acyclic aliphatic substrates (propanal and an ethylphosphonium salt) to generate a simple internal E-alkene. It avoids aromatic groups, which are common in general Wittig examples but could cause collisions with other named reactions involving arenes. The use of a trimethylphosphonium salt instead of the more common triphenylphosphonium salt further reduces aromatic content and focuses on the core transformation. The explicit inclusion of two equivalents of organolithium followed by a proton source highlights the specific conditions of the Schlosser modification, distinguishing it from the standard Wittig reaction.
